seed_problem: Partition a weighted graph into two balanced halves maximizing cut weight; checker validates balance and scores cut/baseline.
mutation: composite: relax the exact bisection to a balance WINDOW (each depot within slack of n/2) AND plant the instance as two id-contiguous material families, each hiding an internal balanced sub-bipartition of heavy conflict edges. The index-split baseline cuts no intra-family weight, so the achievable cut is several times the baseline and requires per-family local search to find; the balance slack adds a trade-off knob, keeping the optimum NP-hard and heuristics divergent.
title: Split the Stream: Twin-Depot Recycling Assignment
strategies: index-split baseline: zones 1..n/2 to depot 0, rest to depot 1 (the calibrated baseline)
sequential greedy: place zones one at a time on the depot that maximizes incremental separated conflict weight, subject to the balance cap
swap-based local search / Kernighan-Lin: from a starting labeling, repeatedly apply the balanced 0/1 swap with the largest cut gain until locally optimal, over several randomized restarts
randomized multi-restart with slack exploitation: seed random balanced labelings, local-search each, and use the balance window to trade a little imbalance for extra separated weight
